item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't usually downvote, but for this one I had to do it.
The concept is pretty okay, but the bad writing and pseudo-science take all the focus away from it.
+First
-First

How the object was acquired, usage of the subobject denominations (1227-A etc.) and names come after the description of the actual object. The way it's written now, you see that it's about some Russian guy and… other guys? Parts of other people? Half of the article is ambiguous because the description doesn't start with the actual description.

+Second
-Second

'Safe zone' is a very unsafe wording for the area where the skip can attack you. "Safety outside the safe zone is usually assured" - then why is it called "safe"? Better call it "the red zone" or sth.

+Other issues
-Other issues

Also, as others pointed out, having more instances of one organ won't make them function like a super-organ. This could be handled in other ways, though, such as cell structure alteration in the incorporated organs.
Last, but not least, the O5 have better things to do than make irrelevant comments on a test result, such as, let's say, keeping the world safe?


From what I've read, this was a first try, so some errors are to be expected, but for the love of SCP-343, at least try to fix them after you get feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Achievement unlocked:

ALL IN THE DAY`S WORK
Make an O5 Council member unreasonably supervise and make pointless commentaries on your SCP, too.

I think i could be neutral or actually upvote this, but people, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Again, feeling neutral.

The concept barebones isn't that interesting, but the psychological angle you give it is interesting enough to keep my interest.

However, my two major problems are the bad science and the pseudo-RSE backstory. The former is just egregious, even for this site, whereas the former is worder in a way that resembles more a rather morbid black comedy than something that makes me feel for this guy or the other prisoners.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is was from, before it was removed, was James Gunn's (Guardians of the Galaxy) //Slither/. It was a horror comedy starring Nathan Fillion, Elizabeth Banks, and Michael Rooker (the actor pictured.) I've no idea about the other removed picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops! accidentally reverted page. Fixed. That was a close one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
New for 2016, it's Flip That Vote!

I feel like this has a lot of similarities to 466, which is overall a better article, but it's got enough going for it to differentiate it. Maybe a little heavy on the psychological profiling, but generally effective and creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but I would like it even more if it was well written. As of now, it has several biological mistakes (he has so much lungs that he can live in a vacuum for months!!1 SCIENCE!) and tonal errors (At the very end it goes from scientific description to unnecesary psichological analisis [He clawed his eyes out to not be able to see the horror!] Both of those things are easily fixed). I'll downvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You articulated my thoughts exactly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I do like how the body horror was handed, I still can't upvote. There are some tonal errors that kinda broke it for me (like how you kept switching between "he" and "it" to describe the SCP) and the wonky biology. It could work if you made it more like "we have no idea how he manages to do it" rather than "he has so many stomachs, he made the ultimate stomach".

The biggest problem was that it reminded me a lot of The Russian Sleep Experiment. Not because I'm saying you copied it, but because seeing a concept like this tends to work only once, so that kinda took the fun away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what?
You get the up vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but its too much like 763. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I downvoted when I reached the part about multiple lungs allowing efficient respiration in vacuum (as ShenHibiki noted above), but when reading the comments, realized how many other fundamental bits of biology the author fucked up.
It is creative (although it DID remind me of the sleep experiment creepypasta), but the science is so sub par, I wish I could give it more than one downvote. That being said, I suspect it could be fixed to actually earn an upvote, but for now, No.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture doesn't really work, the tone isn't Foundation-like enough (You explained how they found it before even a cursory explanation of what it is. Considering these are supposed to be documents read in case of emergency…not a great idea.), and there are parts that should be blacked out but aren't. In spite of that, the idea is solid and I was hooked for the last third. This skip is interesting, gross, and full of Fridge Crying-Your-Eyes-Out. Upvoted, though I hope the problems listed can/will be fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but the picture looks very stupid. I actually think it would be best if this didn't have a picture, so that we could fill in the blanks with our imaginations. Your tone is also a bit weak at times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus Christ this is horrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not an expert but… the fact his respiratory system is 'improved' shouldn't let him survive in vacuum, right?
Starting with not being addapted to the negative pressure, then moving to the fact that in vacuum he can't process anything, so the respiratory system, no matter how good it is, won't help at all… that should be more in the level of a mutation letting his blood store oxigen, maybe?
Still, I think 'vacuum' is too extreme. Is it just me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks everybody for the feedback, especially in identifying the tonal and spelling errors. I'm currently working to improve this. A lot of people don't like the addendum at the end, so I think I'll try and replace it with something better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
10/02/1991 - [DATA EXPUNGED]

I don't like this at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never mind that. 60g of pure , crystalline urea (no, it wouldn't be urine at that concentrations, and I think even birds who deposit urea as white coating on their shit don't reach 99.9 % purity. Fuck, this man would be golden as a factory.) is an absurdly small amount. For reference - half a litre of piss contains , courtesy of wikipedia, roughly 9.3 g of urea - this means that this .. whatever it is excretes urea in amounts that are roughly 50-100x smaller than what a normal person does. Ugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One observed effect is that SCP-1227 urinary tract is now three times longer than normal and so efficient that SCP-1227-F is now observed to deposit roughly 60g of concentrated urine every three years, which analysis has demonstrated to be 99.9% pure urea

This should mention the volume of the kidneys, not the length of the urinary tract, since the kidneys are what produce urine, and the tract just takes the urine from the bladder to the outside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It still needs proofreading. I counted at least 10 typos, most of which were "SCP-1227" instead of "SCP-1227's".
I'm downvoting this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this idea. I've played many a game with characters like it, but it's been done well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.

I like the concept very much, the explanation is very thorough too. It creeps me out just thinking about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I swear that I've read this before, but I enjoy how you thoroughly explained what was happening, and otherwise found this article interesting.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh, dislike this new picture more than the last.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really reminds me of that dude that absorbs people who touch him except in this case there is more body horror. Also the picture is pretty lame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the creepier origin stories that I've read on the site, the rest is interesting as well. I like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed about the mental health; his mood seems more like on the positive side, but that seems to be a result of his poor mental health.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the Russian sleep experiment with a new coat of paint on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Master: http://images.wikia.com/fallout/images/d/d1/FO01_NPC_Master_B.png

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
lead to three further days of incoherent screaming, muttering, torture, rape and one forced abortion.

I laughed my ass off at this line. It's bad writing to have two lists in a row, especially when trying to squick someone.

10/02/1991- [DATA EXPUNGED] I think I speak for all of us when I say we are NEVER trying that again. –O5-█.

This is not the Men In Black. No LoLwhatcouldtheyhavetried? I don't know if it bothers other people, but it sure as hell bothers me when some of these lines feel like they were delivered by Will Smith. Plus, don't expunge the entirety of a log; it's pointless just to add length for length's sake. I also dislike when the Foundation treats objects like human beings. This is not a human being, why are we giving it books!?

Despite the changes SCP-1227 has undergone, his mental health appears adequate.

Then

He appeared to be under the impression that he was ‘preparing a feast’ for his ‘family’ the whole time.

If that's adequate, I would really hate to see what the Foundation considers inadequate mental health.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have serious tonal issues, especially in the last bit. You could use a once-over for your grammar and your word choice, which leave a lot to be desired. However, you have creeped me out enough that I still upvoted, despite these issues. If you fix them, more upvotes will follow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think spelling and grammar has mostly been delt with now. I also feel that the tone has been improved, but if you see any further problems with that, do let me know, because I 'm finding it a lot harder to tell where I'm going wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote, as I can see you're going to fix up mistakes I had qualms with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for all your replys. I'm going to be spending the next hour or so fixing up all those grammar and spelling errors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I'm downvoting for a couple reasons, but if you fix this up, I'll be upvoting.

  1. A lot of atrocious grammar
  2. You describe it in the first, like, three paragraphs of the Description.
  3. Tonal issues ("heavy" winter?)
  4. Too many shitty addenda (crosstest with 294… really? [DATA EXPUNGED]?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great body horror, but I can't upvote due to the various issues stated above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find the new picture to be much more fitting, you have earned your +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have a pretty dire spelling error (assessable, it can be assessed?) in the first sentence. I downvoted because spelling errors like that cause a kneejerk reaction with me. Spelling and grammar can be corrected though, as can tone. And the idea is certainly better than I've seen lately (including my own), so I'll retract that downvote momentarily. I won't be upvoting till you fix the things I mentioned earlier though. Regardless of your standing in this community, if you can't proofread your work (or get it proofread), it's going to hurt you in the long run.

Edit: seems that whatever's ailing this guy struck before: SCP-1119, only that guy doesn't leave an outward trace of those absorbed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After 5 days of relatively normal social functioning, the group descended into two groups, leading to three further days of incoherent screaming, muttering, torture, rape and ████████.

no! not incoherent screaming, muttering, torture, rape and line dancing!

there is nothing that could go there that needs redaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's from The Attack of the Chocolate Bankers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I swear I've never even played Fallout.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've changed the picture! Hopefully you'll find the new one a little better. And yeah, the old one was from a movie, don't know which one- I just put 'alien infection' into google images. In reply to Tl333s, the central SCP-1227 is special because only he can initiate the bonding process, and also because he's the only living sentient creature of the bunch, and all the other bodies only exist to sustain him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the plus side, this is the Master from Fallout, just with a different genesis.
On the plus side ,this is also reasonably well done body horror, which I'm a fan of .

On the minus side, this is the Master from Fallout, just with a different genesis.
On the minus side, the tone and actual science involved is quite horrible.

Result - no vote.

EDIT: On second thought, downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image looks like it's from a movie I've never seen, which is why I downvoted.

Also, why is the SCP identified as a male bonded to nine other people rather than "a bonded group of ten people"? I mean, what makes him so special?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That pic from a movie? I swear I've seen it before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, my main problem is with the image, it doesn't seem to fit, its almost like you are trying to get a cheap scare from the reader right from the off. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1a) its co-operation should not be taken for granted
1b) drawing its body parts around it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its sort of the opposite of 763: this one dystorys the brain function and maintains the bodies, where as 763 melts the bodies into a wierd human mess, and somtimes keeps the brains.

But yeah. the image blows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. "its new situation", not "it's new situation".

2. The image seems far too fake to me, it looks like it's out of some movie and at best is just gratuitous gore.

3. This is uncomfortably close to the Human Beowulf Cluster for me, which feels far more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a great concept, the picture's creepy, but it needs proofreading. It isn't quite enough to downvote, but it's close.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this article, the psychoanalysis bits really add to it.

A few minor annoyances on the biology angle:
1) the reason we piss out so much water is nothing to do with in efficiencies in our internal organs: quite the opposite in fact! Essentially it’s a quick way to get rid of water in the blood, along with all the other nastiness you find in urea. I mean at a stretch you could have one of the bodies hold a stomach into which excess water is placed, but seems a bit odd.
2) extra organs wouldn’t lead to extra respirational efficacy. what you want there is altered cell composition
3)this can prolly be over looked, but muscles and esophagus need to be covered by skin, or some sort of impermeable membrane. they leak all over the carpet and make a mess other wise
But yeah apart from those pedantic picks, seems damn fine!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can be more assertive than that:

Experimental data suggest that this combination of multiple organ systems has greatly incrased SCP-1227's biological efficiency, adapting it to survive on limited resources.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much for your thoughts. I'm working to correct it right now! Just for reference is something like 'it is theorised that the practical functioning is…' better than 'practical upshot'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, some thoughts.

1. For a first time, this is actually a remarkably original idea. Kudos for that.
2. Your principal problem is a lack of clinical tone. It's really not a thing you can teach someone, but phrases like

The team sent to observe the prisoners discovered a large amount of decomposing [DATA REDACTED] with SCP-1227 having formed in the center of it, seemingly in good physical and mental health, greeting the researchers like old friends.

The practical upshot of this is that SCP-1227 is now perfectly adapted to survive on limited resources.

The problem with the first is the bolded part; you effectively never need similes in SCPs, since researchers won't be comparing things to other things nonliterally. The whole tone of the second one (and a frequent tonal issue throughout the SCP) is that it sounds like a sidenote that a researcher wrote about the SCP; it uses terms that scientists would never use ("upshot," "perfectly adapted") and essentially sounds like it's praising the SCP for being a good SCP. Who's a good SCP? SCP-1227's a good SCP! Yes you are!

3. The last addendum goes way too far. You were fine with what you had, before you added in "BTW OMG ITS GONNA HAVE SCP BABIES ITS SPIDERS HE'S TALKING ABOUT MIDWIVES WHAT????" Though I compliment you for not naming the kid "Redips."

4. You might want to tone down the redactions. Here's a guide on when not to redact; that whole addendum that is just a redaction should probably go ASAP. It tells us nothing and turns an otherwise serious article into lolfoundation.

5. The picture? What is that? It looks like it's from a movie, which may or may not be a problem of itself, but it's sort of a problem that it looks like it's from a movie. Is there any other picture you can get?
